mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving from a restrictive toward an easing stance.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is declining over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 2-year IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent trend signals weakening labor income and softer consumption, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if SPX is positive in more than 60% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal demand and revenue momentum, which should lift near-term earnings revisions and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the sign of the relationship flips for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO while HOUST remains weak marks an industrial rebound without broad housing support, a combination that typically reflects late-cycle capacity pressure and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 3 months while HOUST is flat or down over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-HOUST condition does not outperform each series alone, or if the triggered sample has positive 60-day SPX returns more than half the time.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration measured by CPIAUCSL combined with a high FEDFUNDS rate compresses real liquidity and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when policy is already tight.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-tight-policy trigger does not yield negative next-week SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll contraction and weaker household income, which should reduce equity demand and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign remains negative after controlling for UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and tighter private sector cyclicality, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days once construction activity turns down persistently.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in the triggered sample, or if the median forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is improving together, the economy is in synchronized demand expansion that supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive and RSAFS rises over the same recent window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDPC1-RSAFS expansion condition fails to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.
